title: *The Odyssey*, the movie (no real spoilers) - Marginal REVOLUTION
gist: Tyler Cowen reviews the new film adaptation of The Odyssey as a mixed disappointment: strong set-piece scenes but widespread miscasting, weak dialogue, and a disliked ending, despite heavy production investment.

themes.theme: Strengths and weaknesses of the adaptation

insights.claim: Cowen judges the Circe scene, the Cyclops sequence, and the Troy battle scenes as the strongest parts of the new film adaptation of The Odyssey, while judging most of the cast as miscast overall.
insights.speaker: Tyler Cowen
insights.so_what: A specific quality signal on a high-profile literary film adaptation, relevant to box-office and critical-reception expectations for the studio behind it.

insights.claim: Cowen thinks Matt Damon is too tied to his 'Good Will Hunting' persona to convincingly play Odysseus, so the character never cohered on screen, and rates Ralph Fiennes's performance in the rival film The Return as superior.
insights.speaker: Tyler Cowen
insights.so_what: A direct comparative casting critique useful for assessing this adaptation's competitive position against The Return, a rival Odyssey-themed film.
insights.locator: I like Matt Damon, but he is too

insights.claim: Cowen calls Zendaya's role in the film superfluous and considers the Calypso casting the worst element, comparing the character to someone he might meet at a Tupperware party in the Hamptons.
insights.speaker: Tyler Cowen
insights.so_what: Flags specific casting/writing weaknesses that could factor into critical and audience reception.

insights.claim: Cowen judges at least half the film's dialogue as weak and inconsistent with the spirit of the Mycenaean world, and says he strongly disliked the changes made to the ending.
insights.speaker: Tyler Cowen
insights.so_what: Signals script and ending choices as a specific risk factor for the film's critical reception.
insights.locator: At least half of the dialogue was lame

insights.claim: Despite heavy investment of energy, effort, and money that makes the film watchable on the big screen, Cowen concludes it counts as a disappointment relative to critical reviews and prior expectations.
insights.speaker: Tyler Cowen
insights.so_what: A net-negative critical assessment from a high-profile commentator, relevant to expectations for the film's commercial and awards-season performance.
insights.locator: this has to count as a disappointment
